steps.title: Identify the y-intercept
steps.content: All four choices share the constant 148.13, matching the line's y-intercept visible on the graph. The choices differ only in slope.

steps.title: Estimate the slope from the graph
steps.content: At \(x = 10\) the line reads approximately \(y = 735\). The rise from 148.13 to 735 over a run of 10 gives: $$m \approx \frac{735 - 148.13}{10} \approx \frac{587}{10} \approx 58.7$$

steps.title: Match to choices
steps.content: The nearest slope in the choices is 58.61 (choice D). Verify: \(148.13 + 58.61(10) = 734.23 \approx 735\). ✓

explanation:
• Choice A — 'y = 148.13 + 0.02x': Slope of 0.02 is nearly flat; the line on the graph rises sharply. ✗
  • Choice B — 'y = 148.13 + 0.59x': At x = 10, gives 154.03 — far below the graph's endpoint near 735. ✗
  • Choice C — 'y = 148.13 + 48.61x': At x = 10, gives 634.23 — lower than the observed endpoint. ✗
  • Choice D — 'y = 148.13 + 58.61x': At x = 10, gives 734.23 — closely matches the graph endpoint of ~735. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
